item.title: U.S., Iran trade more strikes after Trump says ceasefire is "over"
item.description: The U.S. began a second night of strikes Wednesday and Iran responded in kind after President Trump declared earlier in the day that the ceasefire was "over."

item.title: U.S. military launches second night of strikes against Iran
item.description: The U.S. military launched another round of strikes against Iran late Wednesday night, U.S. Central Command said, in the second night of attacks as diplomacy between the two countries appears to collapse.

item.title: Rahm Emanuel calls to end unconditional U.S. support for Israel in Tel Aviv
item.description: Former Chicago mayor and former U.S. ambassador to Japan Rahm Emanuel spoke at Tel Aviv University on Wednesday. The potential Democratic presidential candidate called to end unconditional U.S. support for Israel as part of a larger political shift among Democrats.

item.title: What to know about "explosive" diarrhea-causing parasite reported in several states
item.description: Michigan is experiencing its largest outbreak of a parasitic infection, called cyclosporiasis, that causes severe diarrhea.

item.title: Judge orders release of over $5 million due to E. Jean Carroll in Trump case
item.description: The damages were awarded to Carroll, a writer, by a 2023 jury in a civil sexual abuse and defamation case.

item.title: Suspect's roommate in Charlie Kirk killing given immunity for recorded statements
item.description: Prosecutors revealed that the roommate of the man accused of assassinating Charlie Kirk was given "use immunity" in exchange for providing recorded video statements to investigators about the case.

item.title: Trump revokes waiver allowing Iranian oil sales
item.description: The rise in oil prices comes after the Trump administration on Tuesday revoked a waiver that allowed Iran to sell oil. CBS News coordinating producer Richard Escobedo has more.

item.title: June was the hottest on record for Western Europe, second hottest globally
item.description: A new report by the Copernicus Climate Change Service also found that June temperatures in Western Europe were nearly 5.5 degrees above average.

item.title: Middle East expert says he does not think the ceasefire with Iran is fully over
item.description: U.S. Central Command said the U.S. military is conducting a new wave of strikes on Iran at the direction of the president. The second night of strikes came after President Trump said the fragile ceasefire between the two countries was over as far as he was concerned. Will Todman, senior fellow for the Middle East Program at the Center for Strategic and International Studies, has more.

item.title: Graham Platner suspends Maine Senate campaign, maintains innocence
item.description: Maine Democratic Senate candidate Graham Platner has suspended his campaign following sexual assault allegations. He has denied all claims. The 41-year-old announced his decision in a social media video on Wednesday. Caitlin Huey-Burns reports.

item.title: Graham Platner suspends Maine Senate campaign after sexual assault allegation
item.description: Maine Democratic Senate candidate Graham Platner is suspending his campaign against GOP Sen. Susan Collins, after a woman accused him of sexually assaulting her five years ago.

item.title: How Democrats could pick a new Maine Senate nominee to replace Graham Platner
item.description: Maine Democrats are facing a ticking clock to find a new candidate to go up against GOP Sen. Susan Collins this fall, after Graham Platner dropped out of the race following a sexual assault allegation.

item.title: Graham Platner drops out of Senate race | Special Report
item.description: Maine Democrat Graham Platner has dropped out of his race for Senate following sexual assault allegations that he continues to deny. The 41-year-old announced his decision in a social media video on Wednesday. Jessi Mitchell anchors this special report.

item.title: Masih Alinejad calls Ayatollah Khamenei's funeral a "terrorist summit"
item.description: New U.S. strikes on Iran come as the Islamic Republic holds the funeral of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. CBS News contributor Masih Alinejad joins with analysis.

item.title: Maine Democrat Graham Platner suspends U.S. Senate campaign
item.description: Maine Democrat announced Wednesday that he's suspending his U.S. Senate campaign after being accused of assault. He continues to maintain his innocence. CBS News' Caitlin Huey-Burns reports.

item.title: Toddler declared dead in pool accident found alive in hospital morgue
item.description: Two police officers saw possible signs of life, but the child was still taken to the hospital's "cold room" after being treated by staff, according to police documents.

item.title: How 3 kids convinced the Michigan House to change a law
item.description: Three brothers from Michigan have run a lemonade stand for the last three summers. After a local Michigan health department said their lemonade stand required a permit, they decided to fight it. Tony Dokoupil has the story.

item.title: New video shows how Arizona toddler was declared dead, but later found alive
item.description: Warning: Disturbing footage. New details and new video show how an Arizona toddler was declared dead hours before a hospital morgue discovered he was actually alive. Jericka Duncan reports on what officers tried to tell the doctor.

item.title: Progressive and moderate Democrats clash in Michigan Senate primary debate
item.description: Michigan state Congresswoman Haley Stevens and former public health official Dr. Abdul El-Sayed did not hold back their personal attacks while squaring off in a tense Senate primary debate on Tuesday. CBS News' Fin Gómez has the details.

item.title: Trump licensing Patriot defense systems to Ukraine is a "very big change," expert says
item.description: At least three people are dead in Ukraine following a new wave of Russian missile and drone strikes overnight. On Wednesday, President Trump said the U.S. will license the Patriot missile defense systems to Ukraine for production. Ian Bremmer, the president and founder of the Eurasia Group, joins "The Daily Report" to discuss.

item.title: 7/8: CBS Evening News
item.description: U.S. announces new strikes on Iran as Trump declares the ceasefire over; Mississippi family demands answers after missing teen found dead.

item.title: 2026 Emmy nominations are announced: See the full list
item.description: The nominations for the 78th annual Primetime Emmy Awards were announced Wednesday morning in Los Angeles, with the final season of HBO Max's "Hacks" setting a new record for the most nominations in a single year for a comedy series.

item.title: Trump says U.S. will grant Ukraine's request to license Patriot defense system
item.description: "A little birdie told me this, about the fact that we'll give them the right to make Patriots," President Trump told Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy.

item.title: U.S. hits dozens of Iranian targets in retaliatory strikes after ship attacks
item.description: With peace talks on hold, the U.S. said it launched a "series of powerful strikes" on Iran in response to Iranian attacks on three commercial vessels in the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Fetterman tells those who backed Platner to "sit it out" on a possible replacement
item.description: Sen. John Fetterman condemned his colleagues who continued backing Graham Platner amid a string of scandals.

item.title: Sen. Bernie Sanders says he encouraged Graham Platner to step aside
item.description: Independent Sen. Bernie Sanders of Vermont said Tuesday that he spoke with Maine Democratic Senate nominee Graham Platner and recommended that he exit the Senate race.

item.title: Top Democrats call on Platner to drop out as sexual assault allegation emerges
item.description: A Maine woman told Politico that Graham Platner entered her home in late 2021 and forced himself on her. Platner denies the allegation.

item.title: 7/5: Sunday Morning
item.description: Hosted by Jane Pauley. Featured: The military newspaper Star and Stripes, actor J.K. Simmons, "Little House on the Prairie" returns to screens, Founding Father Thomas Paine, a visit to George Washington's Mount Vernon, and hifi listening bars.

item.title: The Uplift: America 250
item.description: Celebrate the Fourth of July and America's 250th anniversary with inspiring stories about America's rarest artifacts, most resilient small towns and loyal patriots.

item.title: Graham Platner wins Maine Democratic primary, will face Sen. Susan Collins
item.description: The primary victories set up a race that could be key to Democrats' hopes of winning control of the Senate.
